title: Caprifolium utahense
intro: Caprifolium utahense, also known as Utah honeysuckle or Lonicera utahensis, is a flowering plant species in the Caprifoliaceae family, characterized by its fragrant, showy flowers and twining stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, respiratory issues, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Caprifolium utahense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infused oil, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be extracted and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
taxonomy: Caprifolium utahense is a member of the family Caprifoliaceae, which is commonly known as the honeysuckle family. This family comprises approximately 130 species of plants, primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere. Caprifolium utahense is native to the southwestern region of North America. The plant's classification at the genus level is a part of the larger Caprifolium genus, which consists of a small number of species. The precise classification of Caprifolium utahense is still under review in some botanical sources.
